title: gastrochilus biglandulosus
intro: Gastrochilus biglandulosus, also known as the "Tongue Orchid" or "Snake Orchid," is a type of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Gastrochilus biglandulosus include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Gastrochilus biglandulosus is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the dried roots and rhizomes being steeped in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed for its medicinal benefits.
